title: haworthia mirabilis var. scabra
intro: Haworthia mirabilis var. scabra is a rare and unique succulent species belonging to the Asphodeloideae subfamily, characterized by its striking, rough-textured leaves. Traditionally, the Haworthia mirabilis var. scabra has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing, due to its bioactive compounds like alkaloids and glycosides. In traditional herbal preparations, the Haworthia mirabilis var. scabra is often used to create infusions, decoctions, and ointments that are applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
